title: cxxrtl: bmux does not mask the result
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.36+8 (git sha1 fb4cbfa73, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

CXXRTL's `$bmux` cell implementation does not mask the result with `msb_mask`, which yields the wrong results. I ran into this bug with a more extensive design, so here is a direct RTLIL example (2x1 `$bmux` with hardcoded input value 0xf1) to pinpoint the issue.

yosys script: `test.ys`:
```
read_rtlil << EOT

module \top
  wire width 1 input 1 \S
  wire width 4 output 2 \Y

  cell $bmux $0
    parameter \WIDTH 4
    parameter \S_WIDTH 1
    connect \A {8'11110001}
    connect \S \S
    connect \Y \Y
  end
end

EOT

write_cxxrtl top.cc
``` 

The C++ file `main.cc`:
```c++
#include <iostream>
#include "top.cc"

int main() {
  cxxrtl_design::p_top top;

  // sel = 0
  top.p_S.set(false);
  top.step();
  std::cout << "Y = " << std::hex << top.p_Y.get<uint32_t>() << std::endl;

  // sel = 1
  top.p_S.set(true);
  top.step();
  std::cout << "Y = " << std::hex << top.p_Y.get<uint32_t>() << std::endl;

  return 0;
}
```

Run:

```bash
yosys -s test.ys
clang++ -ggdb -I$(yosys-config --datdir)/include/backends/cxxrtl/runtime/ main.cc -o test
./test
```

### Expected Behavior

```
Y = 1
Y = f
```

### Actual Behavior

```
Y = f1
Y = f
```
code_blocks:
read_rtlil << EOT

module \top
  wire width 1 input 1 \S
  wire width 4 output 2 \Y

  cell $bmux $0
    parameter \WIDTH 4
    parameter \S_WIDTH 1
    connect \A {8'11110001}
    connect \S \S
    connect \Y \Y
  end
end

EOT

write_cxxrtl top.cc
